title: DSDS Token and Token Group Definitions
description: Documentation for individual design tokens and hierarchical token groups. Token values, aliases, and type information live in the authoritative W3C Design Tokens Format (DTCG) file — DSDS documents the purpose, guidelines, and organizational structure only. Token groups are flexible organizational units — a full token collection, a token family (e.g., all color tokens), or a sub-family (e.g., a single hue family). Groups can contain tokens, nested groups, or both, forming a hierarchy that mirrors how design systems organize tokens. The displayName property is omitted from tokens and token groups — the token name itself serves as the display name. Description and status are optional to reduce verbosity at scale. Shared presentational and classificatory properties (status, since, tags, category, aliases, summary, thumbnail, preview, extends, links) are expressed as typed entries in the modular `metadata` array.

token.description: Documentation for a single design token — name, type, use cases, guidelines, and accessibility considerations. The displayName property is absent — the token name is the display name. Description and status are optional. Shared presentational and classificatory properties (status, since, tags, category, aliases, summary, thumbnail, preview, extends, links) are expressed as typed entries in the modular `metadata` array rather than as top-level properties. When a token appears inside a token group, an omitted status metadata entry is inherited from the parent group.

kind.description: Identifies this entity as a token.

name.description: The token name. Unconstrained by a pattern to accommodate DTCG and design tool naming conventions that use dots, slashes, or other separators (e.g., 'color-text-primary', 'color.text.primary', 'color/text/primary'). Also serves as the human-readable display name.

description.description: A description of the token — what it represents, where to use it, and any constraints. Optional; when omitted, tools MAY generate a description from the token name and type.

tokenType.description: The type of token aligned with the DTCG spec (e.g., 'color', 'dimension', 'fontFamily', 'fontWeight', 'duration', 'cubicBezier', 'shadow').

source.description: Reference to the authoritative W3C Design Tokens Format (DTCG) definition. DSDS does not duplicate token values — the DTCG file is the source of truth for resolved values, aliases, and type information.

file.description: URI or relative path to the DTCG file containing this token's definition.

path.description: The dot-path to this token within the DTCG file (e.g., 'color.text.primary').

metadata.description: Modular metadata entries. Each entry is a typed object with a `kind` discriminator. Types include status, since, tags, category, aliases, summary, thumbnail, preview, extends, and links.

documentBlocks.description: All structured docs for this token as an ordered array of typed document block objects. Tokens accept general document block types only (guideline, use-cases, accessibility, examples). Each document block entry has a `type` property that determines its shape. Ordering is significant: tools SHOULD preserve it for display.

tokenGroup.description: A group of related design tokens. Token groups are flexible organizational units — a full collection, a family (e.g., all color tokens), a sub-family (e.g., a single hue with its grade scale), or any other logical grouping. Groups can nest to form a deep hierarchy. A top-level group might represent 'color', containing child groups for 'color-text', 'color-background', and 'color-border', each with individual tokens. This recursive structure mirrors how real design systems organize tokens into families and sub-families. The displayName property is absent — the group name is the display name. Description and status are optional. Shared presentational and classificatory properties (status, since, tags, category, aliases, summary, thumbnail, preview, links) are expressed as typed entries in the modular `metadata` array rather than as top-level properties. Children inherit the parent group's status metadata entry when they omit their own.

kind.description: Identifies this entity as a token-group.

name.description: The token group name. Unconstrained by a pattern to accommodate DTCG and design tool naming conventions (e.g., 'color-text', 'color.text', 'color/text'). Also serves as the human-readable display name.

description.description: A description of the group's purpose, the tokens it contains, and how they relate. Optional; when omitted, tools MAY generate a description from the group name and children.

tokenType.description: When all tokens in this group share the same DTCG token type, declare it here to avoid repetition on each child. Each token in children MAY override this value. Common values: 'color', 'dimension', 'fontFamily', 'fontWeight', 'duration', 'number', 'shadow'.

source.description: Reference to the authoritative W3C Design Tokens Format (DTCG) definition. DSDS does not duplicate token values — the DTCG file is the source of truth for resolved values, aliases, and type information.

file.description: URI or relative path to the DTCG file containing this group's definition.

path.description: The dot-path to this group within the DTCG file (e.g., 'color.text').

children.description: An ordered array of tokens and/or nested token groups in this group. Each item MUST be either a token or a tokenGroup. Ordering is significant — tools SHOULD preserve order for display, as it often represents a meaningful progression (e.g., lightest to darkest, or smallest to largest). Children inherit tokenType and status from this group when they omit their own values.

metadata.description: Modular metadata entries. Each entry is a typed object with a `kind` discriminator. Types include status, since, tags, category, aliases, summary, thumbnail, preview, extends, and links.

documentBlocks.description: All structured docs for this token group as an ordered array of typed document block objects. Accepts general document block types only (guideline, use-cases, accessibility, examples). Document blocks for the group as a whole live here. Document blocks for individual tokens SHOULD be placed on the token within children.
